clusters.headline: Second tanker in three months hijacked off Yemen by suspected Somali pirates
clusters.summary: There have also been several unsuccessful attacks in a region where piracy appears to be making a comeback.

clusters.headline: Air conditioner sparked deadly blaze at children's orphanage - police
clusters.summary: Eleven people, including children, died in the fire that also injured 19 others.

clusters.headline: Uganda suspends school trips after 20 pupils die in bus crash
clusters.summary: Thousands are killed on Uganda's roads each year but this is one of its deadliest accidents involving children.

clusters.headline: Network School ordered to cease operations at Forest City site over licensing breach; founder says operations continue
clusters.summary: Johor Chief Minister Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the Iskandar Puteri City Council has issued a cessation notice to one of the startup community's premises as well as an advertising licence notice for displaying non-compliant signboards.

clusters.headline: Liberia: Civil Society Council Challenges Suspension of H/Rights Commission Chair, Cites Constitutional Violations
clusters.summary: [FrontPageAfrica] Monrovia -- The National Civil Society Council of Liberia (NCSCL), along with its membership-based Human Rights Coalitions, has gravely criticized President Joseph Nyuma Boakai's decision to suspend the Chairperson of the Independent National Commission on Human Rights (INCHR), Cll

clusters.headline: Negeri Sembilan election campaign begins after 103 candidates confirmed; BN, PN to campaign for each other
clusters.summary: The 36 constituencies will see 11 straight fights, 21 three-cornered contests, two four-cornered contests and two five-cornered contests.

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Ododo Reaffirms No-Ransom Policy As Security Forces Rescue Abducted Neco Candidates in Kogi
clusters.summary: [Leadership] Kogi State governor, Alhaji Ahmed Usman Ododo, has reiterated his administration's policy of not negotiating with kidnappers or paying ransom, following the successful rescue of four persons abducted from a NECO examination centre in Dekina Local Government Area of the state.

clusters.headline: Approved fosterers may keep up to 6 cats in HDB flats, 10 in private homes under new pilot scheme
clusters.summary: The two-year pilot scheme will run from Aug 3, 2026 to Jul 31, 2028.

clusters.headline: Congo-Kinshasa: In Eastern DR Congo, Journalists Face Death, Arson Threats for Reporting On War
clusters.summary: [CPJ] Government allied Wazalendo militiamen have threatened to kill or capture one reporter and to burn down another's radio station because of unfavorable coverage, as frontlines shift under diplomatic pressure for peace in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo.

clusters.headline: Indian activist moved to hospital after 20-day hunger strike to protest country’s exam system
Trump alleges China meddled in 2020 election and questions voting security ahead of midterms
clusters.summary: China said Trump's claims were fabricated and US Democrats said he was paving the way to undermine November's elections.

clusters.headline: China's Xi calls for step up of global effort in AI, as US curbs squeeze China's tech access
clusters.summary: American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.
